question: What happens if you create yup.array() without calling .of()?

options.text: Yup only checks that the value is an array but does not validate elements
Error — of() is required for array()
options.explanation: of() is not required, but without it Yup doesn't check array contents

options.text: Each element will be validated as mixed()
options.explanation: Without of(), elements are not validated at all — only the value being an array is checked

options.text: The array will be converted to an empty array
options.explanation: array() without of() accepts any array with any elements without conversion

options.text: Because required() checks that the array is not null/undefined, and an empty array is a valid value

options.explanation: This is expected behavior: an empty array [] is not null or undefined
required() works with arrays but only checks for null/undefined, not emptiness

options.text: of() cancels the effect of required()
options.explanation: of() and required() are independent checks. To reject empty arrays, add min(1)

question: Which statements about tuple() in Yup are correct?

options.text: Each tuple element can have its own type
label() helps make error messages more understandable
tuple() takes an array of schemas, one for each position
tuple() automatically coerces element types like array()
options.explanation: Tuples do not have automatic type coercion by default, unlike array()

question: What does the ensure() method do for arrays?

options.text: Guarantees the value becomes an array: null -> [], undefined -> [], single value -> [value]
Checks that the array is not empty
options.explanation: Use min(1) to check non-emptiness. ensure() is a transformation that wraps non-arrays into an array
ensure() does not remove duplicates. It guarantees the value will be an array

options.text: Checks that all elements are unique
options.explanation: ensure() is a transformation for coercing values to arrays, not a uniqueness validation
